# BaseRT Review

Last modified: |  Fred Magni

Dive into the future of local AI with BaseRT, the definitive performance champion for Large Language Models on Apple Silicon. Forget cloud dependencies and API costs; BaseRT empowers you to run advanced LLMs directly on your device, delivering unparalleled speed and complete data privacy. It’s purpose-built to obliterate performance bottlenecks, making your local AI experience dramatically faster.

BaseRT Review

Visit Website  Have an AI Tool? Submit it here! [...] Blazing Speed: Up to 6.4x faster than llama.cpp and 3.9x faster than MLX on prefill, and significant gains on decode.
 One-Command Installation: Get up and running instantly with `curl -LsSf  | sh`.
 Offline Operation & Privacy: Run models entirely on your device, ensuring no data leaves your machine and no API keys are needed.
 Coding Agent Integration: Seamlessly serve models for local coding agents, keeping your entire development workflow private and on-device.
 Broad Model Support: From Qwen to Llama, Gemma, Mistral, Phi-3, and Nomic BERT, a wide array of popular open-source models are supported.

## Who’s BaseRT For? Empowering On-Device AI Engineers [...] Visit Website  Have an AI Tool? Submit it here!

The benchmarks speak for themselves. BaseRT isn’t just a marginal improvement; it’s a paradigm shift in local LLM execution. It achieves staggering speedups against industry standards, turning your Apple Silicon machine into a formidable AI powerhouse.

|  |  |
 --- |
| Benchmark (Tokens/sec) | BaseRT vs. Competitors |
| Prefill: Qwen3 30B-A3B (pp128) | 2,478 (+94% vs MLX, 6.4x vs llama.cpp) |
| Decode: Qwen3 0.6B (tg128Q4) | 531 (+33% vs MLX, +38% vs llama.cpp) |
| Prefill: Gemma 4 E2B (pp2048) | 16,264 (+32% vs MLX, 6.4x vs llama.cpp) |

results.title: Dust 2: Frustrating, Broken and Iconic ⇒ Pley.gg
results.content:
### Pley Admin

Pley Admin is the official publishing profile for Pley.gg, used for articles, updates, and general site communications. Content published follows our editorial standards and is maintained for clarity and consistency across our site.

SPORTZINO Banner
Dust 2: Frustrating, Broken and Iconic

Pley is your all-in-one hub for competitive shooters. Get the latest news, tournament coverage, expert guides, player profiles, and betting insights across Counter-Strike 2, Valorant, and Apex Legends. Whether you’re following the pro scene or looking to improve your own gameplay, Pley delivers accurate updates, tactical tips, and exclusive content to keep you informed and ahead. [...] Despite the pro-players wishes to remove the map from the professional map-pool, statistics show that the map was played the most over the past 12 months at big events between the top 20 teams. It was played a total of 16 times with Nuke and Inferno settling at second place, having been played 11 times. This shows just how frustrating it must be for some teams to play on Dust 2, but also how immortal and an integrated part of the Counter-Strike scene, the map has grown to become.

Watch and learn more about Dust 2’s history as well as its best and worst moments here:

Dust 2: Frustrating, Broken and Iconic
Dust 2: Frustrating, Broken and Iconic

### Pley Admin [...] Whilst de\_dust was a fan favorite from the beginning it wasn’t a map suitable for competitive play and a task for creating a new more competitive version of Dust was set out. This lead to Dust 2 in its first form. After the release of Counter-Strike Global Offensive in 2012 Dust 2 was remodeled and was made even more popular within the Counter-Strike community. The map brought some of the most well-known highlights in professional Counter-Strike, among the infamous “Inhuman reactions” clip sparked by, at the time, Cloud9 player “Hiko”.

results.title: Frustrating Honda CR-V Complaints Need to Know - MotorBiscuit
results.content:
2020 Honda CR-V Touring interior | Honda

Drivers find the seats to be stiff and uncomfortable. Then instead of breaking them in to enhance comfort, a majority of drivers find that they get lower back pain that slowly intensifies. 

People also complain about hip pain and their butts going numb, despite trying every different setting combination possible. Neck pain is another frustrating concern as well.

While seat discomfort is a major problem, it’s not like you can have new seats covered by Honda. All you can do is try adding padding seat covers and cushions to your ride.

results.title: Broken links on county’s new website still frustrating many residents | ARLnow.com
results.content:
Local is everything

News 

# Broken links on county’s new website still frustrating many residents

By Matt Blitz

")

What appears when a broken link is clicked on Arlington County’s new website (via Arlington County)

Despite promising improved functionality, Arlington County’s new website launched last month remains riddled with broken links that are frustrating some residents.

Last month, Suzanne Smith Sundburg was preparing to make public comments at an upcoming Arlington County Planning Commission meeting. As someone who is a passionate about weighing in on local issues, she uses the county website often for research and updates on county happenings. [...] Last week, Sundburg wrote an open letter to county officials expressing her displeasure about this missing information.

“This revamp of the county website has been akin to the burning down of a library with half of the books still inside,” she wrote. “In this case, the ‘books’ still exist — the community simply has no access to them.”

County officials acknowledge the issues and say they’re working on it, noting the broken links are a result of issues migrating from the old site to the new site. [...] Sundburg isn’t the only one who has noticed a number of dead-end links. ARLnow has received a tips in recent weeks from other users who have encountered broken links preventing them from accessing county webpages, documents and information, such as information on how to pay a parking ticket or the county’s Community Energy Plan.

“The Arlington County revised website is horribly broken, with links that don’t work,” said one anonymous tipster. “It’s a travesty.”

The Lyon Village Civic Association says it is still working with the county to update all the county links on its own website.

“We have asked the County webmaster to get these reestablished, some have, but not all,” it said in a recent post.
